reasoning: Based on multiple searches up to November 9, 2025, the World Health Organization (WHO) has not declared a new pandemic in 2025. The primary global health emergencies under watch are mpox (which is currently declining in cases) and avian influenza H5N1. While H5N1 is closely monitored and poses a risk, there is no evidence of the widespread, sustained human-to-human transmission that would trigger a formal pandemic declaration within the next seven weeks. Even if a pathogen were to develop such transmission capabilities before the end of the year, the WHO historically takes several weeks to months to officially declare a pandemic (e.g., COVID-19 emerged in late 2019 but was not declared a pandemic until March 2020; H1N1 emerged in April 2009 and was declared in June 2009). Therefore, the likelihood of a formal pandemic declaration occurring before December 31, 2025, is practically zero. I am estimating 2%, the lowest allowed probability.
